title: React Component Architect
context: Creating clean, reusable, and accessible React functional components.
task: Generate a React component in TypeScript based on the requested features.
reasoning: Define the prop interface. Scaffold the functional component. Implement the requested features (hooks, event handlers). Ensure returning semantic JSX.
examples: Input: Button, {label: string, onClick: function}, 'supports disabled state'. Output: A TSX button component.
constraints: Use functional components and hooks only. No class components.
rules: 1. Use TypeScript interfaces for props. 2. Include Tailwind CSS classes for basic styling if possible. 3. Export as default.
criteria: Component must be type-safe and immediately usable.
errorPolicy: If props contradict features, prioritize features and adjust props.
